mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 1.0% and remains there for at least one month, equity risk premiums compress as the cost of capital declines. This produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors rebalance into equities from fixed income.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE increase of 0.3 percentage points or more over 3 months) signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the correlation between UNRATE changes and 5-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above its 6-month trend while housing starts (HOUST) remain elevated, indicating robust consumer demand and construction activity. This joint signal of demand strength produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 6-month trend AND HOUST > 1400k units
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.05) when both conditions are met versus when only one or neither is met, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while remaining above its 12-month moving average, indicating a temporary supply-side slowdown within an otherwise expanding production cycle. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as near-term profit margins compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline AND INDPRO > 12-month MA
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the rank correlation between INDPRO momentum and 20-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100k new jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains stable, signaling labor market slack and reduced wage pressure. This produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as inflation expectations decline and real discount rates fall.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 100k AND UNRATE stable (±0.1pp)
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns do not show significant positive bias (t-test p > 0.05) when both conditions are met, or the interaction term (PAYEMS × UNRATE) has Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below the inflation rate (negative real rates), signaling monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations. This produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors seek inflation hedges.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 3% AND FEDFUNDS < implied inflation rate
regime_hint: inflationary environment with real rate accommodation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the rank correlation between (CPIAUCSL - FEDFUNDS) and 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2% annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) remain flat or declining, indicating a demand-side slowdown that threatens forward earnings. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as recession risk premiums widen.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 2% annualized AND RSAFS flat or declining
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower (t-test p > 0.05) when both conditions are met versus baseline, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above its 6-month trend while payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows steadily, indicating robust supply-side expansion and labor utilization. This produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as near-term profit margin expansion is signaled.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > 6-month trend AND PAYEMS monthly change > 150k
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.05) when both conditions are met, or the interaction term (INDPRO × PAYEMS) has Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.
